response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software engineer with a strong track record across web, mobile, and embedded software, but the role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU/AI accelerator architecture, and SIMD/MIMD optimization — none of which appear in the resume. His AI-adjacent work at Naver is at the application tooling layer (MCP frameworks, agent workflows), not hardware or deep-learning infrastructure. His low-latency work at Sociomantic is relevant to software performance but does not translate to the chip-level performance analysis this position requires.
strengths: ~17 years of diverse software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency systems, meeting the Bachelor's + 8yr education path
Demonstrated software performance optimization skills (high-throughput HTTP services handling millions of requests/second at Sociomantic using async I/O and fiber-based concurrency)
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and tooling at Naver, showing adaptability toward AI-adjacent domains
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, which is the primary technical requirement of the role
No background in hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or AI accelerator workloads
AI involvement is limited to application-layer tooling (MCP code generators, agentic frameworks), not deep-learning training/inference optimization or hardware performance analysis

key_factors.factor: No hardware architecture or GPU/accelerator background
